content.text:
[Subagent Context] You are running as a subagent (depth 1/1). Results auto-announce to your requester; do not busy-poll for status.

[Subagent Task]

You are the Rust specialist. Implement an LRU cache in Rust following the shared spec EXACTLY.

READ FIRST: /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/lru/SPEC.md — follow it precisely (i64 keys/values, O(1) ops, the 7 unit tests, and the EXACT benchmark: capacity 10000, 1,000,000 ops, LCG state uses wrapping u64 arithmetic with the given constants seed 42, KEYSPACE 20000, checksum = wrapping-sum of successful-get values as u64).

TOOLCHAIN: Rust is installed via rustup but NOT on PATH. You MUST source it first in every shell command:
  source "$HOME/.cargo/env"
Then cargo/rustc work. Verify with: source "$HOME/.cargo/env" && cargo --version

Work only inside /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/lru/rust/
Set up a cargo project there (cargo init --name lru_cache). No external crates (std only) — implement the doubly linked list via a Vec-based arena with index "pointers" (Option<usize>) to keep it safe and O(1), or use a HashMap + slotmap-style arena you build yourself. Keys/values i64. get returns Option<i64>.

Deliverables:
- src/lib.rs : LRUCache implementation + the 7 unit tests as #[cfg(test)] mod tests.
- src/main.rs (or a bench binary / example) : the exact benchmark using std::time::Instant. Print total ops, elapsed seconds, ops/sec, final size, checksum.
- Build release: `source "$HOME/.cargo/env" && cargo build --release`
- Run tests: `cargo test` (all must pass).
- Run benchmark with the RELEASE binary (cargo run --release) — do NOT benchmark a debug build.
- RESULT.md : exact commands, test output, benchmark numbers (ops/sec, elapsed, checksum, final size).

Report back concise: tests pass?, benchmark ops/sec, checksum. Retry/fix on failure before reporting.

Begin. Execute the assigned task to completion.
I'll start by reading the spec, then set up the Rust project.

# LRU Cache — Shared Specification (all languages)

Every language implementation MUST match this contract so results are comparable.

## Semantics
- Fixed capacity `N` (> 0) set at construction.
- `get(key)`:
  - If present: return the value AND mark key as most-recently-used.
  - If absent: return a "not found" signal (language-idiomatic: Option/optional/None/null/sentinel).
- `put(key, value)`:
  - Insert/update. Mark most-recently-used.
  - If inserting a NEW key would exceed capacity, evict the least-recently-used key FIRST.
  - Updating an existing key does NOT evict.
- All operations must be **O(1) average** (hashmap + doubly linked list, or equivalent).
- `len()` / size accessor.

## Key/Value types
- Key: 64-bit integer (int64 / i64 / long / int).
- Value: 64-bit integer.
(Keeps benchmarks apples-to-apples; no string hashing noise.)

## Required unit tests (same cases in every language)
1. put/get basic hit.
2. get on missing key returns not-found.
3. Update existing key changes value, no eviction.
4. Exceeding capacity evicts the LRU key.
5. get() refreshes recency (the got key survives a subsequent eviction).
6. Capacity 1 edge case.
7. Overwrite refreshes recency.

## Required benchmark (identical workload)
- Capacity = 10_000.
- Total operations = 1_000_000.
- Deterministic PRNG (seed = 42), simple LCG so every language uses the SAME sequence:
    state = 6364136223846793005 * state + 1442695040888963407  (wrapping, unsigned 64-bit)
    key   = (state >> 33) % KEYSPACE      where KEYSPACE = 20_000  (=> ~50% hit rate)
  For each op: if (state & 1) == 0 -> get(key) else -> put(key, key)
- Warm up with one full pass (not timed), then time one full pass.
- Print: total ops, elapsed seconds, ops/sec, final cache size, and a checksum
  (sum of all successful-get values, wrapping u64) so we can verify all langs agree.

## Output artifact
Each specialist writes `lru/<lang>/RESULT.md` with: build cmd, test result, benchmark numbers, checksum.
Now let me set up the cargo project.

arguments.command: source "$HOME/.cargo/env" && mkdir -p /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/lru/rust && cd /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/lru/rust && cargo init --name lru_cache 2>&1 && ls -la

path = "src/main.rs"

[profile.release]
opt-level = 3
lto = true
codegen-units = 1

arguments.content:
# LRU Cache — Rust Result

Implementation: `std`-only. HashMap<i64, usize> + doubly linked list built on a
`Vec`-based arena with `Option<usize>` index "pointers" and a free list for slot
recycling. 100% safe Rust (no raw pointers). All ops O(1) average.

- Keys/values: `i64`
- `get(key) -> Option<i64>` (marks MRU)
- `put(key, value)` (insert/update, evicts LRU tail first on new-key overflow)
- `len()` accessor

## Toolchain

Rust via rustup, sourced per-shell (not on PATH):

```
source "$HOME/.cargo/env"
cargo --version   # cargo 1.96.1 (356927216 2026-06-26)
rustc --version   # rustc 1.96.1 (31fca3adb 2026-06-26)
```

## Build (release)

```
source "$HOME/.cargo/env"
cd /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/lru/rust
cargo build --release
```

`[profile.release]`: opt-level=3, lto=true, codegen-units=1.

## Tests

```
source "$HOME/.cargo/env"
cd /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/lru/rust
cargo test
```

Output:

```
running 7 tests
test tests::test_capacity_one ... ok
test tests::test_exceed_capacity_evicts_lru ... ok
test tests::test_get_missing_returns_none ... ok
test tests::test_get_refreshes_recency ... ok
test tests::test_overwrite_refreshes_recency ... ok
test tests::test_put_get_basic_hit ... ok
test tests::test_update_existing_no_eviction ... ok

test result: ok. 7 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out
```

All 7 required unit tests pass.

## Benchmark (release binary)

Spec: capacity 10_000, 1_000_000 ops, LCG (mul=6364136223846793005,
inc=1442695040888963407, wrapping u64, seed=42), key=(state>>33)%20000,
even state -> get / odd -> put. One untimed warm-up pass, then one timed pass.
checksum = wrapping-sum (u64) of successful-get values.

```
source "$HOME/.cargo/env"
cd /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/lru/rust
cargo run --release
# or: ./target/release/lru_cache
```

Output (representative; two consecutive runs shown — deterministic checksum/size):

```
=== LRU Cache Benchmark (Rust) ===
total ops      : 1000000
elapsed seconds: 0.046630
ops/sec        : 21445445
final size     : 10000
checksum       : 2470781676
```

```
=== LRU Cache Benchmark (Rust) ===
total ops      : 1000000
elapsed seconds: 0.046397
ops/sec        : 21553057
final size     : 10000
checksum       : 2470781676
```

## Numbers

| Metric        | Value                |
|---------------|----------------------|
| total ops     | 1,000,000            |
| elapsed       | ~0.0464 s            |
| ops/sec       | ~21.5 million        |
| final size    | 10,000               |
| checksum      | 2470781676           |

Tests: PASS (7/7). Benchmark: ~21.5M ops/sec. Checksum: 2470781676 (stable across runs).

content.text: Successfully wrote 2642 bytes to /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/lru/rust/RESULT.md

Done. Rust LRU cache complete.

**Summary:**
- **Tests: PASS** — all 7 required unit tests green (`cargo test`).
- **Benchmark ops/sec:** ~21.5 million (elapsed ~0.0464s for 1,000,000 timed ops after an untimed warm-up pass).
- **Checksum:** `2470781676` (stable/deterministic across runs).
- **Final size:** 10,000.

**Implementation notes:**
- `std`-only, no external crates. HashMap<i64, usize> + doubly linked list built on a `Vec`-based arena using `Option<usize>` index pointers and a free list for slot recycling — 100% safe Rust, all ops O(1) average.
- Keys/values `i64`; `get -> Option<i64>` marks MRU; `put` evicts LRU tail first on new-key overflow, updates don't evict.
- Release build uses opt-level=3, lto=true, codegen-units=1; benchmark run via the release binary.
- LCG matches spec exactly (wrapping u64, seed 42, `(state>>33) % 20000`, even→get/odd→put).

Files in `/home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/lru/rust/`: `src/lib.rs` (impl + tests), `src/main.rs` (benchmark), `Cargo.toml`, `RESULT.md`.
